ClinicalDocument.title: Continuity of Care Document - Owen Garcia

value.displayName: for persistent vomiting to enable oral rehydration
if amoxicillin failure or recent antibiotic use

tr.td: Laceration to forearm from bicycle fall
Worsening cough, new fever after cold
Congestion, runny nose, mild cough for 3 days

act.text: Well-child examination - 10 year old
- [education] Anticipatory guidance provided - [follow-up] Return for next well-child visit

code.originalText: Laceration to forearm from bicycle fall

act.text:
10-year-old male presents after falling off bicycle approximately 1 hour ago. Was riding on sidewalk, hit a crack, and fell onto concrete. Landed on outstretched right arm. Sustained laceration to right forearm. No loss of consciousness, no head strike, was wearing helmet. Denies numbness/tingling in hand or fingers. Moving all digits well. Bleeding controlled with pressure by parents.
PMH: Type 1 Diabetes, Asthma. No bleeding disorders. Immunizations up to date including tetanus (DTaP at 4 years, Tdap at age 10).
Laceration of forearm, right: 4cm linear laceration, clean edges, requires suture repair Abrasions, right hand and knee: Superficial, cleaned and dressed Bicycle accident

- [procedure] Wound irrigation with normal saline - [procedure] Laceration repair with 4-0 nylon sutures, 8 sutures placed - [medication] Lidocaine 1% with epinephrine for local anesthesia - [medication] Ibuprofen 400mg PO for pain (given in office) - [medication] Ibuprofen 400mg PO Q6H PRN pain x 3 days - [education] Wound care instructions provided: Keep dry x 24 hours, then may shower. No soaking or swimming. - [education] Signs of infection to watch for: increasing redness, warmth, swelling, drainage, fever - [other] Tetanus up to date - no booster needed - [follow-up] Return in 10-12 days for suture removal - [education] Monitor blood sugars - stress can cause elevation
type_1_diabetes follow-up type_1_diabetes, stable
- [medication] Continue current medication regimen - [follow-up] Return in 3-6 months for condition review

10-year-old male with Type 1 Diabetes (diagnosed age 4) and asthma (diagnosed age 2) presents for combined chronic disease management follow-up.
DIABETES: Using insulin pump (Omnipod), doing well with current settings. Downloading pump data shows time in range 65%, slightly below goal of 70%. Some afternoon lows noted, may need basal adjustment. Using Dexcom G7 CGM, calibrating regularly. Last HbA1c was 7.4% (goal <7.5%). No episodes of DKA or severe hypoglycemia. Sees endocrinology every 3 months.
ASTHMA: Well-controlled on current regimen. Using fluticasone 44mcg 2 puffs BID and albuterol PRN. Rescue inhaler use 1-2x per week, usually with exercise. Recent cold 2 months ago triggered mild exacerbation treated with oral steroids. No ED visits or hospitalizations this year. Peak flows at baseline.

Type 1 Diabetes mellitus - stable, fair control: HbA1c 7.4%, time in range 65%. Some afternoon hypoglycemia. Asthma - well-controlled: Minimal rescue inhaler use, no nocturnal symptoms, no activity limitation.

- [medication] Continue current insulin pump settings; decrease afternoon basal by 0.05 units/hr from 2-5pm to address lows - [order] HbA1c today - target <7.5% - [order] Comprehensive metabolic panel - [order] Thyroid panel (annual screening) - [order] Celiac panel (annual screening) - [order] Urine microalbumin/creatinine ratio - [referral] Annual ophthalmology exam due - referral placed - [education] Reviewed sick day rules, ketone management - [medication] Continue fluticasone 44mcg 2 puffs BID - [medication] Continue albuterol MDI 2 puffs PRN - [education] Reviewed asthma action plan - updated copy provided - [education] Pre-treat with albuterol 15 min before exercise - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for diabetes follow-up - [follow-up] Endocrinology appointment in 6 weeks

code.originalText: Worsening cough, new fever after cold

act.text:
Patient was seen 9 days ago for upper respiratory infection symptoms. Initially improved over first 4-5 days, but over past 2-3 days developed worsening cough that is now productive. New fever to 102.4F starting yesterday. Decreased activity level, not wanting to eat. Some increased work of breathing noted by parents. Blood sugars running higher than usual despite increased insulin. Increased albuterol use to every 4 hours for cough/wheeze.
Prior encounter ID: 7a134448

Community-acquired pneumonia, right lower lobe: Secondary bacterial pneumonia following viral URI. Focal findings on exam, clinical syndrome consistent with CAP. Asthma exacerbation, mild: Triggered by respiratory infection Type 1 Diabetes with illness-related hyperglycemia

- [medication] Amoxicillin 90mg/kg/day divided BID x 5 days (1372mg BID) - [medication] Continue albuterol nebulizer Q4H while awake x 48-72 hours, then transition back to MDI PRN - [medication] Prednisone 1mg/kg daily x 5 days for asthma exacerbation - [order] Chest X-ray ordered to confirm pneumonia - [education] Increase insulin by 10-20% during illness with steroid use - [education] Check blood sugars every 3-4 hours, check ketones if BG >300 - [education] Push fluids, rest - [follow-up] Recheck in 48-72 hours or sooner if worsening - [other] Return immediately or go to ED if: increasing work of breathing, SpO2 <92%, unable to take oral medications, persistent vomiting, or lethargy

code.originalText: Congestion, runny nose, mild cough for 3 days

act.text: 10-year-old male presents with 3-day history of nasal congestion, clear rhinorrhea, and mild dry cough. Low-grade fever to 100.2F yesterday. Decreased appetite but drinking fluids well. No ear pain, sore throat, or difficulty breathing. Parents gave ibuprofen for fever with good response. History of asthma - no increase in rescue inhaler use. Type 1 diabetes - blood sugars slightly elevated but no ketones.
Acute upper respiratory infection, viral: Typical viral URI presentation. No bacterial superinfection.

- [medication] Continue ibuprofen PRN for fever/discomfort - [medication] Saline nasal spray as needed for congestion - [education] Increase fluid intake, rest - [education] Monitor blood sugars more frequently during illness - [education] Continue current asthma and diabetes medications - [other] Return if fever persists >5 days, develops ear pain, difficulty breathing, worsening cough, or blood sugar concerns
asthma follow-up asthma, stable
- [medication] Continue current asthma regimen - [other] Asthma well controlled, continue current management - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for asthma review
Insect Bite Reaction
- [other] Supportive care with rest and hydration - [follow-up] Return if symptoms worsen or do not improve in 3-5 days
type_1_diabetes follow-up type_1_diabetes, stable
- [medication] Continue current medication regimen - [follow-up] Return in 3-6 months for condition review
Rash
- [other] Topical care as appropriate for rash type - [education] Return if rash spreads, becomes painful, or child develops fever
Allergic Rhinitis
- [other] Supportive care with rest and hydration - [follow-up] Return if symptoms worsen or do not improve in 3-5 days
asthma follow-up asthma, stable
- [medication] Continue current asthma regimen - [other] Asthma well controlled, continue current management - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for asthma review
type_1_diabetes follow-up type_1_diabetes, stable
- [medication] Continue current medication regimen - [follow-up] Return in 3-6 months for condition review

Patient presents for routine 9-year well-child examination. Parents report child is doing well overall. Active in school and sports. Managing diabetes with insulin pump therapy. Asthma well-controlled on current regimen.
Well-child visit Type 1 Diabetes - stable, well-controlled Asthma - well-controlled on current therapy

- [medication] Continue current insulin regimen via pump - [medication] Continue fluticasone inhaler 44mcg 2 puffs BID - [medication] Continue albuterol PRN for rescue - [order] HbA1c ordered - last was 7.2% - [education] Reviewed diabetes management, carb counting - [education] Reviewed asthma action plan, trigger avoidance - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for diabetes follow-up
Well-child examination - 9 year old
- [education] Anticipatory guidance provided - [follow-up] Return for next well-child visit
Upper Respiratory Infection

- [medication] Acetaminophen 410mg Q4-6H PRN PRN - [medication] Ibuprofen 270mg Q6-8H PRN PRN - [education] Supportive care with rest and increased fluids - [education] Saline nasal drops and bulb suction for congestion - [education] Honey (if >1 year) for cough relief - [education] Return if: difficulty breathing, fever >3 days, ear pain, or worsening symptoms
type_1_diabetes follow-up type_1_diabetes, stable
- [medication] Continue current medication regimen - [follow-up] Return in 3-6 months for condition review
asthma follow-up asthma, stable
- [medication] Continue current asthma regimen - [other] Asthma well controlled, continue current management - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for asthma review
asthma follow-up asthma, stable
- [medication] Continue current asthma regimen - [other] Asthma well controlled, continue current management - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for asthma review
type_1_diabetes follow-up type_1_diabetes, stable
- [medication] Continue current medication regimen - [follow-up] Return in 3-6 months for condition review
Allergic Rhinitis
- [other] Supportive care with rest and hydration - [follow-up] Return if symptoms worsen or do not improve in 3-5 days
type_1_diabetes follow-up type_1_diabetes, stable
- [medication] Continue current medication regimen - [follow-up] Return in 3-6 months for condition review

asthma follow-up asthma, stable
- [medication] Continue current asthma regimen - [other] Asthma well controlled, continue current management - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for asthma review
Viral Gastroenteritis

- [medication] Ondansetron 4mg x1 dose PRN PRN - [education] Oral rehydration with small frequent amounts (1-2 tsp every 5 minutes) - [education] Use Pedialyte or similar oral rehydration solution - [education] Advance diet as tolerated - no need for BRAT restriction - [education] Return if: unable to keep fluids down, no urine in 8 hours, blood in stool, or appears very ill
type_1_diabetes follow-up type_1_diabetes, stable
- [medication] Continue current medication regimen - [follow-up] Return in 3-6 months for condition review

- [medication] Continue current asthma regimen - [other] Asthma well controlled, continue current management - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for asthma review

- [medication] Continue current medication regimen - [follow-up] Return in 3-6 months for condition review
asthma follow-up asthma, stable
- [medication] Continue current asthma regimen - [other] Asthma well controlled, continue current management - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for asthma review
Fever
- [medication] Acetaminophen or ibuprofen for temperature control - [education] Monitor for source of infection, return if fever persists >3 days - [follow-up] Follow up as needed if symptoms worsen
asthma follow-up asthma, stable
- [medication] Continue current asthma regimen - [other] Asthma well controlled, continue current management - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for asthma review

asthma follow-up asthma, stable
- [medication] Continue current asthma regimen - [other] Asthma well controlled, continue current management - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for asthma review
Conjunctivitis

- [medication] Erythromycin Ophthalmic Ointment per package Apply 0.5 inch ribbon QID x 7 days - [education] Antibiotic drops for bacterial; supportive care for viral - [education] Good hand hygiene to prevent spread - [education] Do not share towels, pillowcases, or eye makeup - [education] May return to school 24 hours after starting treatment if bacterial - [education] Return if: vision changes, severe pain, or photophobia
asthma follow-up asthma, stable
- [medication] Continue current asthma regimen - [other] Asthma well controlled, continue current management - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for asthma review

- [other] Supportive care with rest and hydration - [follow-up] Return if symptoms worsen or do not improve in 3-5 days

- [medication] Acetaminophen 290mg Q4-6H PRN PRN - [medication] Ibuprofen 190mg Q6-8H PRN PRN - [education] Supportive care with rest and increased fluids - [education] Saline nasal drops and bulb suction for congestion - [education] Honey (if >1 year) for cough relief - [education] Return if: difficulty breathing, fever >3 days, ear pain, or worsening symptoms

Evaluation for asthma symptoms asthma, newly diagnosed

- [medication] Start albuterol inhaler PRN for rescue - [education] Asthma education provided: triggers, inhaler technique, action plan - [referral] Consider pulmonology referral if poorly controlled - [follow-up] Return in 3 months for asthma review
Well-child examination - 4 year old
- [education] Anticipatory guidance provided - [follow-up] Return for next well-child visit
Scald burn to hand
Upper Respiratory Infection

- [medication] Acetaminophen 220mg Q4-6H PRN PRN - [medication] Ibuprofen 150mg Q6-8H PRN PRN - [education] Supportive care with rest and increased fluids - [education] Saline nasal drops and bulb suction for congestion - [education] Honey (if >1 year) for cough relief - [education] Return if: difficulty breathing, fever >3 days, ear pain, or worsening symptoms
Well-child examination - 3 year old
- [education] Anticipatory guidance provided - [follow-up] Return for next well-child visit
Acute Otitis Media

- [medication] Amoxicillin 1250mg divided BID x 10 days - [medication] Amoxicillin-Clavulanate 1250mg divided BID x 10 days - [medication] Ibuprofen 140mg Q6H PRN PRN - [education] Complete full course of antibiotics - [education] Ibuprofen or acetaminophen for pain control - [education] Return if no improvement in 48-72 hours - [education] Return immediately for high fever, facial swelling, or severe pain
Upper Respiratory Infection

- [medication] Acetaminophen 200mg Q4-6H PRN PRN - [medication] Ibuprofen 140mg Q6-8H PRN PRN - [education] Supportive care with rest and increased fluids - [education] Saline nasal drops and bulb suction for congestion - [education] Honey (if >1 year) for cough relief - [education] Return if: difficulty breathing, fever >3 days, ear pain, or worsening symptoms

Croup

- [medication] Dexamethasone 6mg x1 dose x 1 days - [education] Single dose of dexamethasone is typically curative - [education] Cool mist or night air may provide temporary relief - [education] Keep child calm - crying worsens stridor - [education] Return IMMEDIATELY for stridor at rest, drooling, or respiratory distress
Well-child examination - 11 month old
- [education] Anticipatory guidance provided - [follow-up] Return for next well-child visit

tr.td: No developmental concerns at this time
